title: EBS default encryption should be enabled
description: This control checks whether Amazon Elastic Block Store (EBS) encryption is enabled by default. The control fails if EBS default encryption is not enabled.
what_is_wrong: This control checks whether Amazon Elastic Block Store (EBS) encryption is enabled by default. The control fails if EBS default encryption is not enabled.
what_the_fix_does: Enables EBS default encryption for newly created volumes.

findings.title: EBS default encryption should be enabled

recommendation.rationale: Matrix cell risk_medium__criticality_high maps to default recommendation 'pr_only'. Risk is medium and business criticality is high. Criticality evidence: data_sensitivity=0.70, privilege_level=1.00, internet_exposure=0.00, matched_signals=none. Relationship context is incomplete, so the recommendation remains conservative and advisory unless policy overrides it. Current execution surfaces stay PR-only, so this recommendation remains advisory until the reviewed bundle is applied.

execution_guidance.label: Generate PR bundle for default EBS encryption (AWS managed key)
execution_guidance.blast_radius_summary: Affects account-wide security posture for account 696505809372.

pre_checks.message: Confirm this change is intended for AWS::::Account:696505809372 in account 696505809372 (us-east-1).
Confirm the repository, deployment path, and approver that own this infrastructure change before generating the PR bundle.
Default encryption applies to newly created volumes; existing volumes are unchanged.

execution_guidance.expected_outcome: PR mode produces a reviewable infrastructure change: All new EBS volumes in this region will be encrypted by default. After merge and apply through your IaC path, Security Hub should reflect the remediated state within 12-24 hours.

post_checks.message: Review the generated bundle or diff, then capture plan and apply evidence after the change is merged through the normal infrastructure workflow.
Verify the completed change only affected the intended account scope for AWS::::Account:696505809372.
Trigger immediate re-evaluation after the change lands and confirm the action closes without reopening.

rollback.summary: Use this rollback path to revert the infrastructure change through the same reviewed delivery workflow.
notes: Prefer reverting through version control and your normal deployment pipeline so rollback stays auditable.
